objective: Build a simple Python weather CLI app that has separate modules for API calls and data formatting.
history: State: PLANNING - Specification created. Moving to planning...
State: INTEGRATION - Execution complete. Moving to integration...

tasks.title: Implement CLI Module
tasks.description: Develop the CLI module in main.py to parse command-line arguments to get the city name and coordinate between different modules. Key function to be implemented is get_city_from_args().

tasks.title: Create API Module
tasks.description: Develop the API module in weather_api.py to handle interactions with the weather API. Key functions to implement are fetch_weather_data(city: str) and construct_api_url(city: str).

tasks.title: Develop Data Formatting Module
tasks.description: Create the Data Formatting module in formatter.py to format raw weather data into a human-readable format. Implement the function format_weather_data(data: dict).

tasks.title: Select and Integrate Weather API
tasks.description: Research and choose a reliable weather API (e.g., OpenWeatherMap or WeatherAPI). Obtain an API key and integrate it within the API module.

tasks.title: Set Up Development Environment
tasks.description: Configure the development environment for Python 3.x and ensure the requests library is installed for handling HTTP requests.

tasks.title: Write Unit Tests
tasks.description: Write unit tests for each module to ensure correct functionality, including checking edge cases such as invalid city names and API errors.

tasks.title: Document Codebase
tasks.description: Add PEP-257 compliant docstrings to each function and module. Prepare README.md with usage instructions and setup guidelines.
